summary:
The role of microbial plaque is clearly linked in chronic and juvenile periodontitis, necrotizing ulcervative gingivitis, but it is notably obscured in desquamative gingivitis due to an undefined relationship between infection and disease pathogenesis. Desquamative gingivitis has no definitive causal connection to specific microorganisms or plaque accumulation unlike other options presented.

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{C}}$

The role of microbial plaque is best understood in periodontal diseases and necrotizing ulcerative gingivitis, both of which have strong associations with pathogens such as *Fusobacterium* species. Conversely, in desquamative gingivitis, there is insufficient evidence establishing a direct causal link between microbial plaque and pathology, making this condition unique for having the most obscure role of plaque. 

$\boxed{\text{C}}$  

Only one summary should be included at the end — do not repeat summaries multiple times.

The role of microbial plaque is well-defined in periodontitis and necrotizing ulcerative gingivitis but poorly established in desquamative gingivitis due to lack of conclusive evidence linking plaque directly to disease onset or progression. Thus, microbial plaque’s contribution is most obscure in desquamative gingivitis.  
\boxed{\text{C}}
